paper_title: Compliance of cleaned frustules: Static vs. dynamic approach

methods.method_name: Analytical Model Fitting for Membrane Deformation
methods.description: This method involves using an analytical model for circular membrane deformation to determine the apparent Young's modulus of a diatom valve by fitting the model to experimental observations.

steps.description: The data obtained was used in combination with the analytical model for circular membrane deformation given by Melnikov.
steps.searchable_summary: combine experimental data with analytical model for membrane deformation

steps.description: By fitting the model to experimental observations, the membrane flexural rigidity can be used to determine the overall apparent Young's modulus of the diatom valve as a circular membrane.
steps.searchable_summary: fit model to determine Young's modulus

methods.description: This method calculates the compliance of the diatom valve as a measure of deformation under external forces and analyzes its dependence on various factors.

steps.description: The compliance defined as the measure of the structure deformation under the action of external forces was calculated as the reciprocal of rigidity.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate compliance as reciprocal of rigidity

steps.description: The dependence of compliance on the relative radial position calculated using influence function of a point force is shown in Fig. 6b.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze compliance dependence on radial position

steps.description: Based on the dependence of the displacement on time, considering sinusoidal motion represented as A·sin(ωt), we found the stiffness and subsequently compliance as a function of cycle number.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze compliance as function of cycle number using sinusoidal motion

methods.description: This method analyzes the local material compliance and global structural compliance of the diatom valve under different loading conditions.

steps.description: The total compliance of the system can be written as the sum of the local material compliance under the indenter, C L , and the global structural compliance C G.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total compliance as sum of local and global compliance

steps.description: The local compliance decays with increasing applied force as C L ∼ = F −m , whilst the global compliance C G remains appreciably constant.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze local compliance decay with force

steps.description: Both local and global effects can be considered together only once the global compliance has been calibrated.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate global compliance for combined analysis
